Module contents

Sustainability metrics data models.

class core.sustainability.models.CarbonIntensityFactors(country_code: str, kg_per_kwh: float, source: str, source_url: str, year: int, data_quality: str = 'medium', uncertainty_percent: float = 10.0, metadata: Dict[str, ~typing.Any]=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Grid carbon intensity factors for a country.

Scientific Notes:

  • Values vary by country based on energy mix

  • Data quality indicates confidence in reporting

  • High quality: Official government statistics

  • Medium quality: Industry reports

  • Low quality: Estimates or interpolations

country_code

ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code [Req 2.1]

Type:

str

kg_per_kwh

Carbon intensity in kg CO₂e/kWh [Req 2.1]

Type:

float

source

Data source name [Req 2.16]

Type:

str

source_url

Verifiable URL [Req 2.16]

Type:

str

year

Data year

Type:

int

data_quality

“high”, “medium”, “low” [Req 2.16]

Type:

str

uncertainty_percent

Reported uncertainty

Type:

float

metadata

Additional context

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

country_code: str
data_quality: str = 'medium'
kg_per_kwh: float
metadata: Dict[str, Any]
property quality_score: float

Convert quality to numeric score (0-1).

source: str
source_url: str
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Convert to dictionary.

to_json() → str[source]

Serialize to JSON.

uncertainty_percent: float = 10.0
year: int
class core.sustainability.models.CarbonMetrics(kg: float, uncertainty_percent: float = 10.0, confidence_level: float = 0.95, per_query_mg: float | None = None, query_count_validated: bool = False, source: str = 'Ember 2026', source_url: str | None = 'https://ember-energy.org/data/global-electricity-review-2026/', year: int = 2026, calculation_method: str = 'energy_kwh × grid_carbon_intensity', timestamp: float = <factory>, metadata: Dict[str, ~typing.Any]=<factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Carbon footprint metrics for a single measurement with uncertainty.

Scientific Notes:

  • Carbon intensity varies by grid region and time of day

  • Uncertainty reflects grid reporting accuracy (±5-15%)

  • 95% confidence intervals are standard for GHG reporting

  • Per-query metrics require query_count validation

kg

Carbon dioxide equivalent in kilograms [Req 2.4]

Type:

float

uncertainty_percent

Estimated uncertainty (±%) [Req 2.16]

Type:

float

confidence_level

Statistical confidence level (default 0.95)

Type:

float

per_query_mg

Carbon per query in milligrams [Req 2.9]

Type:

float | None

source

Source of grid carbon intensity data [Req 2.1]

Type:

str

source_url

Verifiable URL for data source [Req 2.16]

Type:

str | None

year

Data year

Type:

int

calculation_method

How this was derived

Type:

str

timestamp

When calculation was performed

Type:

float

metadata

Additional context (grid region, time of day)

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

calculation_method: str = 'energy_kwh × grid_carbon_intensity'
confidence_level: float = 0.95
property grams: float

Carbon in grams (for human readability).

property grams_lower: float

Lower bound in grams.

property grams_upper: float

Upper bound in grams.

property interval_width_percent: float

Width of confidence interval as percentage.

kg: float
property kg_lower: float

Lower bound of confidence interval.

property kg_upper: float

Upper bound of confidence interval.

metadata: Dict[str, Any]
property milligrams: float

Carbon in milligrams (for per-query metrics).

property milligrams_lower: float

Lower bound in milligrams.

property milligrams_upper: float

Upper bound in milligrams.

per_query_mg: float | None = None
query_count_validated: bool = False
source: str = 'Ember 2026'
source_url: str | None = 'https://ember-energy.org/data/global-electricity-review-2026/'
summary() → str[source]

Human-readable summary with confidence intervals.

timestamp: float
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][source]

Convert to dictionary for serialization [Req 2.16].

to_json() → str[source]

Serialize to JSON with pretty formatting.

uncertainty_percent: float = 10.0
validate_query_count(query_count: int) → None[source]

Validate that per-query metrics are meaningful.

Parameters:

query_count – Number of queries in measurement

Raises:

ValueError – If query_count <= 0

Scientific Note:

Per-query metrics assume energy measured corresponds exactly to the number of queries specified.

year: int = 2026
class core.sustainability.models.MethaneLeakageFactors(country_code: str, g_per_kwh: float, source: str, source_url: str, year: int, data_quality: str = 'medium')[source]

Bases: object

Grid methane leakage factors for a country.

country_code

ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code

Type:

str

g_per_kwh

Type:

float

source

Data source

Type:

str

year

Data year

Type:

int

data_quality

“high”, “medium”, “low”

Type:

str

data_quality: str = 'medium'

source: str
source_url: str

year: int
class core.sustainability.models.MethaneMetrics(kg: float, co2e_20yr: float | None = None, co2e_100yr: float | None = None, source: str = 'IEA Methane Tracker 2026', source_url: str | None = 'https://www.iea.org/reports/global-methane-tracker-2026', year: int = 2026, gwp_source: str = 'IPCC AR6', gwp_source_url: str = 'https://www.ipcc.ch/report/ar6/wg1/chapter/chapter-7/', calculation_method: str = 'energy_kwh × grid_methane_leakage')[source]

Bases: object

Methane emissions metrics for a single measurement.

kg

Methane emitted in kilograms

Type:

float

co2e_20yr

CO₂ equivalent using 20-year GWP

Type:

float | None

co2e_100yr

CO₂ equivalent using 100-year GWP

Type:

float | None

source

Source of methane leakage data

Type:

str

year

Data year

Type:

int

gwp_source

Source for GWP values (IPCC AR6)

Type:

str

calculation_method

How this was derived

Type:

str

calculation_method: str = 'energy_kwh × grid_methane_leakage'

co2e_20yr: float | None = None

Methane in grams.

class core.sustainability.models.WaterMetrics(liters: float, per_query_ml: float | None = None, source: str = 'UN-Water 2025', source_url: str | None = 'https://www.unwater.org/publications/un-world-water-development-report-2025', year: int = 2025, calculation_method: str = 'energy_kwh × grid_water_intensity')[source]

liters

Water consumed in liters

float

float | None

str

int

str
